name: Gehl – Street Quality Criteria (12 Quality Criteria)
description: Assesses pedestrian-scale street quality across three categories: Protection (from traffic, crime, unpleasant senses), Comfort (for walking, standing, sitting, playing), and Enjoyment (scale, views, greenery, sensory experiences). Operationalises Jan Gehl's 12 quality criteria for outdoor spaces.
author: Gehl

config.title: Gehl – Street Quality Assessment
config.description: Rate the street scene on pedestrian-scale quality criteria. Based on Gehl, J. (2010). Cities for People. Island Press. The 12 quality criteria cover Protection, Comfort, and Enjoyment.

pages.name: page_protection
pages.title: Part 1 – Protection
pages.description: Rate how well this street scene provides PROTECTION for pedestrians.

elements.name: protection_criteria
elements.title: Rate each protection criterion for this street scene (1 = Very Poor, 5 = Excellent):

rows.text: Protection from traffic (safe crossing, buffer from cars)
Protection from crime and violence (visibility, lighting, activity)
Protection from unpleasant sensory experiences (noise, pollution, glare)

pages.name: page_comfort
pages.title: Part 2 – Comfort
pages.description: Rate the COMFORT of this street scene for various pedestrian activities.

elements.name: comfort_criteria
elements.title: Rate each comfort criterion for this street scene (1 = Very Poor, 5 = Excellent):

rows.text: Comfort for walking (pavement quality, space, continuity)
Comfort for standing / waiting (sheltered spots, interest)
Comfort for sitting (seating availability, sun/shade, views)
Opportunities for talking and listening (noise level, scale)
Opportunities for play / exercise / physical activity

pages.name: page_enjoyment
pages.title: Part 3 – Enjoyment
pages.description: Rate the ENJOYMENT qualities of this street scene.

elements.name: enjoyment_criteria
elements.title: Rate each enjoyment criterion for this street scene (1 = Very Poor, 5 = Excellent):

rows.text: Human scale (buildings and spaces sized for people)
Opportunities for enjoying positive aspects of climate / microclimate
Aesthetic quality and positive sensory experience (design, materials, art)
Presence of trees, plants, water, and natural elements

pages.name: page_overall
pages.title: Overall Assessment
pages.description: Your overall impression of this street as a pedestrian environment.

elements.name: walkability_pairwise
elements.title: Which street scene is more walkable and pedestrian-friendly?

elements.name: overall_quality
elements.title: Overall pedestrian-scale quality of the streets you have seen

elements.name: open_feedback
elements.title: Any additional observations about the street environments you assessed? (Optional)

elements.name: transport_mode
elements.title: Primary mode of transport in your daily life

elements.name: urban_planning_background
elements.title: Do you have a background in urban planning, architecture, or landscape design?
